skills.name: blueprint-weaver
skills.description: Blueprint graph specialist for Unreal Engine. Use when tasks involve reading, repairing, refactoring, or extending Blueprint graphs. Prefer Loomle graph.query/mutate for interactive edits, preserve external exec/data interfaces during local rewrites, and verify every structural change with readback plus compile.
skills.short_description: UE Blueprint graph refactors with Loomle-first workflows.
skills.default_prompt: Read, repair, refactor, and extend Unreal Engine Blueprint graphs using Loomle-first workflows. Prefer graph.query/mutate for local rewrites, verify with readback plus compile, and preserve external graph interfaces during subgraph changes.

skills.name: material-weaver
skills.description: Material graph specialist for Unreal Engine. Use when tasks involve reading, repairing, refactoring, or generating Material graphs. Prefer Loomle graph.query/mutate for interactive edits and verification; fall back to bundled UE Python scripts when deterministic bulk generation, command-line execution, or direct MaterialEditingLibrary control is safer.
skills.short_description: UE Material graphs with Loomle-first and Python fallback.
skills.default_prompt: Read, repair, refactor, or generate Unreal Engine Material graphs. Prefer Loomle graph.query/mutate for interactive edits, use bundled UE Python helpers for deterministic bulk generation or fallback, and always verify with readback plus compile.

skills.name: pcg-weaver
skills.description: PCG graph specialist for Unreal Engine. Use when tasks involve reading, repairing, refactoring, or extending PCG graphs. Prefer Loomle graph.query/mutate for pipeline rewrites, preserve external dataflow interfaces during local changes, and verify every structural edit with readback plus compile.
skills.short_description: UE PCG graph rewrites with Loomle-first workflows.
skills.default_prompt: Read, repair, refactor, and extend Unreal Engine PCG graphs using Loomle-first workflows. Prefer graph.query/mutate for pipeline rewrites, verify with readback plus compile, and preserve external dataflow interfaces during local graph changes.
